mechanism_narrative: Near-zero federal funds rates compress discount rates applied to future corporate earnings, directly supporting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >65% of months where FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index readings exceeding 280.0 signal entrenched inflation that forces central banks to accelerate rate hikes, compressing equity multiples and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 280.0, or the signal loses predictive power when inflation expectations are anchored below 3%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous payroll employment growth exceeding 300,000 and unemployment falling below 4.0% indicates extreme labor market tightness that compresses corporate profit margins through wage inflation, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Corporate profit margins expand or remain stable despite these labor conditions, or 60-day forward returns are positive in >55% of triggered quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Real retail sales and housing starts both registering positive month-over-month growth confirm robust consumer demand that sustains short-term equity momentum, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Short-term returns turn negative in >70% of weeks where both series show positive growth, or the signal fails to predict direction when supply chain constraints dominate price formation.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production falling below the 2017 base index of 100.0 signals manufacturing contraction and supply-side bottlenecks that reduce corporate output and earnings, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Earnings growth remains positive or 20-day returns are positive in >60% of months where INDPRO drops below 100.0, or the threshold effect disappears in rolling backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly gross domestic product growth above 2.0% in chained dollars provides a fundamental earnings floor that supports equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in >65% of quarters where GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.0%, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 across all tested macro regimes.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of CPIAUCSL above 280.0 and FEDFUNDS below 0.50% creates a policy divergence that forces abrupt central bank tightening, triggering sharp equity drawdowns and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Markets price in gradual easing or 20-day returns remain positive in >55% of months where this divergence persists, or the interaction term shows no statistical significance in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate crossing above 4.5% signals labor market deterioration that increases expectations for central bank accommodation, triggering short-term relief rallies and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Rising unemployment triggers sustained bear markets instead of relief rallies, or 5-day returns are negative in >65% of weeks where UNRATE exceeds 4.5%, or the signal fails during liquidity crises.
